title: lomaria sellowiana
intro: Lomaria sellowiana, also known as the Sellow's fern or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. Native to South America, this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Lomaria sellowiana include reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
